stories.title: PGA Championship Preview — McIlroy Chases Back-to-Back Majors, Scheffler Defends

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
PGA Championship starts THURSDAY. 🏆

McIlroy won the Masters in April. A win this week would make him the first to win the first two majors of the year since Spieth in 2015.

Scheffler defends. Young is World No. 3. Big week. #PGAChampionship

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Aronimink Golf Club. Par 70. 176 bunkers. Donald Ross design.

The PGA Championship venue hasn't hosted golf's second major since Gary Player won here in 1962.

64 years of waiting. Starting Thursday. 🏌️ #PGAChampionship

facebook.long_form:
Two days from now, the best golfers in the world tee it up at Aronimink Golf Club in Pennsylvania for the PGA Championship — and the storylines heading into this week might be the best we've seen at a major in years.

Rory McIlroy arrives as the Masters champion, fresh off defending his green jacket in April to win his sixth major title. He's seeking to become the first player since Jordan Spieth in 2015 to win the first two majors of the year. He's been here before, and he's as hungry as he's ever been.

Scottie Scheffler is the defending champion — but the World No. 1 has been the bridesmaid more than the bride in 2026, finishing runner-up three straight times (Masters, RBC Heritage, Cadillac Championship). He skipped the Truist last week specifically to prepare for Aronimink.

Cameron Young arrives at World No. 3 after winning the Players Championship and Doral, still chasing his first major win and playing the best golf of his life.

Then there's the course itself. Aronimink hasn't hosted the PGA Championship since 1962, when Gary Player won. The Donald Ross design features 176 bunkers, massive bentgrass greens, and four par-3s over 210 yards. Approach play is going to matter enormously.

First tee shots are Thursday. This one is going to be good.
facebook.image_caption: PGA Championship starts THURSDAY. McIlroy defends two Masters titles and eyes back-to-back majors. Scheffler wants redemption. Young is hot. Aronimink awaits. 🏆 #PGAChampionship

article.headline: PGA Championship Preview: McIlroy Chases Back-to-Back Majors, Scheffler Seeks Redemption at Aronimink

stories.title: Cameron Young's PGA Championship Moment — World No. 3, Two Wins, Zero Majors

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Cameron Young in 2026:
→ Won the Players Championship
→ Won Doral by 6 shots
→ World No. 3
→ Zero major wins

The PGA Championship at Aronimink starts Thursday. His best chance yet. #PGAChampionship

facebook.long_form:
Ask yourself this: which active PGA Tour player has been better in 2026 than Cameron Young?

The 27-year-old from Scarborough, New York won the Players Championship in March in dramatic fashion — birdying the island green 17th and watching Matt Fitzpatrick bogey the 18th to hand him the title. Then he went to Trump National Doral and won the Cadillac Championship wire-to-wire, finishing 19-under and winning by six shots over Scottie Scheffler.

He enters this week's PGA Championship as the world's No. 3 ranked golfer, the highest ranking of his career.

There is just one thing Young has not yet done: win a major championship.

Aronimink Golf Club may be the place where that changes. CBS Sports projects approach play as the most important statistical category this week, given the course's sweeping 8,300-square-foot greens and the need for precision over power. Young's game — particularly his iron play — fits that profile.

He's won two of his last six starts. He's playing the best golf of his life. Thursday can't come soon enough for Cameron Young fans.
facebook.image_caption: Players Champion. Doral Winner. World No. 3. Cameron Young arrives at his best PGA Championship yet with zero major wins but everything to gain. #PGAChampionship

article.headline: Cameron Young Is Playing the Best Golf of His Life — Can He Win His First Major at Aronimink?

stories.title: Nelly Korda 4 Points From LPGA Hall of Fame — Queen City Preview

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Nelly Korda is 4 points away from the LPGA Hall of Fame.

She tees it up THIS WEEK at the Kroger Queen City Championship in Cincinnati (May 14-17).

A win = 2 more points. The countdown is ON. 🏌️‍♀️ #LPGA #NellyKorda

facebook.long_form:
Nelly Korda is 4 points away from the LPGA Hall of Fame.

Let that sink in for a moment. The best women's golfer on the planet — a player who has not finished outside the top 2 in six starts this season — is within reach of one of the most prestigious honors in the sport.

Korda currently has 23 of the 27 points needed for automatic LPGA Hall of Fame qualification. She has 3 wins in 2026 already, including the Chevron Championship in April. She is the youngest American to reach 18 career LPGA wins since Nancy Lopez did it in 1980.

And this week, she tees it up in Cincinnati at the Kroger Queen City Championship (May 14-17 at Maketewah Country Club). A win this week would be worth 2 points — putting her at 25 of 27.

She doesn't have to win it in one shot. But the closer she gets, the louder the conversation becomes. Korda has made the Hall of Fame feel inevitable this year. The only question is when.
facebook.image_caption: Nelly Korda: 23 of 27 LPGA Hall of Fame points. Plays THIS WEEK in Cincinnati. A win = 25/27. The countdown is real. 🏌️‍♀️ #LPGA #NellyKorda

article.headline: Nelly Korda Is 4 Points From the LPGA Hall of Fame — Queen City Championship Is This Week

stories.title: Reitan's Truist Win Opens PGA Field — How He Earned His Way to Aronimink

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Two weeks ago: Kristoffer Reitan had never won on the PGA Tour.

This past Sunday: He shot 15-under to win the Truist Championship at Quail Hollow by 2.

This Thursday: He tees it up at the PGA Championship. 🇳🇴 First-time winner to major. #PGAChampionship

facebook.long_form:
One week you win for the first time on the PGA Tour. The next week, you're in the field at a major championship. That's exactly what happened to Kristoffer Reitan.

The 28-year-old Norwegian capped an emotional win at the Truist Championship at Quail Hollow last Sunday — his first PGA Tour victory in his 15th start — with a 2-under 69 to finish at 15-under 269, edging Rickie Fowler and Nicolai Hojgaard by two shots to claim the $3.6 million check from the $20 million purse.

That win punched Reitan's ticket to the 2026 PGA Championship at Aronimink Golf Club, starting this Thursday. In the span of eight days, he went from first-time Tour winner to major championship competitor.

Other notables from Quail Hollow: Cameron Young posted a stunning 9-under 63 in the third round before being unable to close. Rory McIlroy, who many expected to challenge for a rare Quail Hollow title, finished around T19 after a final-round 67. Matt Fitzpatrick was the runner-up threat alongside Fowler and Hojgaard.

For Reitan, it's all gravy from here. The major is a bonus that rewards a breakthrough week.
facebook.image_caption: Kristoffer Reitan: PGA Tour win his 15th start. One week later, he's in the PGA Championship field. From Quail Hollow to Aronimink. 🇳🇴 #Truist #PGAChampionship

article.headline: From First Win to Major Debut: How Kristoffer Reitan Went From Quail Hollow to Aronimink in One Week

stories.title: LIV's PGA Championship Contingent — 11 Players Including Rahm, DeChambeau and Johnson

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
11 LIV Golf players are in the PGA Championship field this week.

That includes Jon Rahm (LIV points leader, 779 pts), Bryson DeChambeau, Dustin Johnson — and Lucas Herbert, who just won at LIV Virginia last week fighting the flu. 🏌️

#PGAChampionship

facebook.long_form:
When the PGA Championship begins Thursday at Aronimink Golf Club, 11 players from LIV Golf will be in the starting field — and some of them arrive in excellent form.

Jon Rahm leads the LIV Golf individual standings with 779 points after two victories in 2026. He's the most decorated player in LIV's short history, a former World No. 1 who won the Masters in 2023 and the US Open in 2021.

Bryson DeChambeau is second in the LIV standings at 476.90 points and arrives healthy after withdrawing with a wrist injury earlier in the spring. He won the US Open in 2020 and finished runner-up at several subsequent majors — major courses suit his game.

Then there's Lucas Herbert. The Australian just won last week's MAADEN LIV Golf Virginia event in wire-to-wire fashion, posting 24-under while battling the flu throughout the tournament. That victory earned him a US Open spot at Shinnecock Hills. He arrives at Aronimink with fresh momentum, a title in his legs, and absolutely nothing to lose.

Dustin Johnson — the 2016 US Open champion — is also in the field.

The crossover between LIV and the majors remains one of golf's most interesting storylines. Aronimink will add another chapter.
facebook.image_caption: 11 LIV players are at the PGA Championship. Rahm (2 wins, LIV leader), DeChambeau, Herbert (won LIV Virginia last week), Dustin Johnson. The majors bring everyone together. 🏌️ #PGAChampionship

article.headline: LIV's 11: Rahm, DeChambeau, Herbert and the LIV Contingent Arrive at Aronimink
